title: Number and string types
instruction:
TypeScript has `number`, `string`, and `boolean` as its core primitive types. Annotate and use them.

Run this.
successMessage: TypeScript's primitive types map directly to JavaScript's runtime types.

title: Arrays
instruction:
Declare a typed array using `type[]` syntax. TypeScript ensures every element matches the declared type.

Run this.
successMessage: Array methods like `filter` preserve the type — `scores.filter(...)` still returns `number[]`.

title: Tuples
instruction:
A tuple is a fixed-length array where each position has its own type. Great for pairs and structured data.

Run this.
successMessage: Tuples enforce both the length and per-position types. TypeScript will error if you add a third element or swap types.

title: The unknown type
instruction:
`unknown` is a safer alternative to `any`. You must narrow the type before using it.

Run this.
successMessage: `unknown` forces you to check the type before using the value — much safer than `any` which skips all checks.
